falsification_conditions.condition: The S&P 500 closes above the 200-day moving average by 5% within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: This would indicate a strong bullish trend, contradicting the current HOLD signal and invalidating the bottom pattern assumption.

falsification_conditions.condition: Volume spikes to 50% above the 200-day average volume for three consecutive days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Such a spike would suggest a blowoff top is occurring, directly contradicting the current blowoff score of 41 and the HOLD signal.

falsification_conditions.condition: The S&P 500 hits a new all-time high with a 10% surge in a single day
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: This would confirm a bullish breakout, falsifying the current unknown verdict and proving the pattern is not a bottom but a continuation of an uptrend.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
